This is very accurate to how people feel in this market.

It's hard to put on a brave face when revenue is down, no one wants your product, you keep facing criticism from CT, and the VC money is running out.

Many teams are announcing they're "winding down."
Others are still imitating social activity.
Some are pivoting.

In truth, none of them can be universally good actors – because there's no realistic way to be a good actor in a bear market.

At some point, these days will be over, too.
And I really hope that moment comes soon.

tweets.text:
Around $2.10B worth of investor ownership in private funds and companies currently exists in transferable tokenized form.

And one product makes up almost half of that entire category.

The BCAP fund, managed by @blockchaincap and tokenized through @Securitize, is valued at around $971.6M, accounting for roughly 46% of the market.

It is one of the three largest assets in the space, alongside the Titan III Equity Token and Hera I Equity Token, both issued through @CtrlAltCo.

Together, those three products make up around 72% of the category’s total market value.

Pretty insane when you think about it, but this is still a very early-stage market.

And I think that shows the tokenized RWA narrative is still being carried by a few large issuances rather than broad institutional adoption.

tweets.text: The biggest mistake young founders make is a variant of this: to build something you imagine people want, instead of studying them and figuring out what they actually do want. But the hack for beating this is to roll with your solipsism and make something for yourself.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: My guess is that the biggest mistake that young adults make is to be overly self-focused. It is fine to be selfish, most folks are, but a mistake to think you serve yourself best by focusing that much on how you feel, look, etc.

thread_tweets.text: This would not ordinarily work, but it happens to work with technology because the young are also the bellwethers. So if you make something you want, other people will magically turn out to want it too.
thread_tweets.full_text: This would not ordinarily work, but it happens to work with technology because the young are also the bellwethers. So if you make something you want, other people will magically turn out to want it too.

thread_tweets.text: This is presumably one reason people can succeed at starting startups so young, when you have to be older to do well at most other similarly complicated things. In startups the usually fatal solipsism of the young can be turned to your advantage.

tweets.text:
Crypto wasn’t invented for the age we came from.

When Satoshi launched Bitcoin, we thought we were building a hedge against banks. Digital gold, permissionless money for humans who couldn't access banks.

All true. But the humans found it hard. Keys, custody, UX... none of it felt natural.

Turns out we were building the base layer for something else entirely.

Agents don't struggle with keys, and smart contracts are native to machines. Every UX problem we've spent years trying to solve was a problem because the users hadn't arrived yet.

Now they have. The agentic economy needs money that moves at its speed, and crypto is the only system that does.

Had the greatest time sitting down with @ErikVoorhees  a few weeks ago to dig into where crypto fits in the agentic world. As ever, please enjoy!

J Cal: Trump Accounts Could Save the American Dream

@Jason:

“In this country, we have a K-shaped recovery going on.

We have immense tension between the haves and the have-nots to the point at which people actually believe that socialism and communism is a better operating system than the best operating system humanity has ever created, which is called democracy plus capitalism, right?

And kids love capitalism. They love building businesses, but we are in an existential moment right now.

Young kids, and there's a couple of generations of them right now who do not believe in America anymore, while we're sitting here on the 250th anniversary of this amazing experiment known as America, (Trump Accounts) are the most American thing you can do.

This is the most beautiful gift I've ever seen to a country, and this could be something that's a unifying principle that brings us back together as a country, that everybody gets to participate in capitalism.

This is like the perfect version of the giving pledge because you're not just saying, ‘I'm giving away my wealth by the time I die,’ you're very strategically saying, ‘Every single person in America gets to be part of the best part of America, which is entrepreneurship, and everybody will be part of the equity nation.’

And we have a large number of people in this country who do not feel safe, and the reason they don't feel safe is because they don't think their kids are safe, to the point at which people do not want to have kids in this country because they feel the system is just too hard. This could change that.

If people feel, ‘Hey, kids have a shot, and I don't have to worry about my kids..’ I worry about my kids and I'm affluent. I can't imagine being a single parent and what anxiety you must have as a single mother or father, and you're making minimum wage and you're behind the eight ball for your entire life, and now your kids are set?

That's all people want. That's the only thing a parent wants, is to make sure their kids have a better future.

That was the promise of this country, and somehow it went off the rails for the last two generations. This puts it back on the rails.”

thread_tweets.text:
In May, he sold STRC to get USD, and then immediately used this USD to buy BTC. This USD did not go in the 'USD Reserve' first, before being used to buy BTC.

Also, the "rule" about the USD reserve only being used to fund div payments is recent, it did not exist back in May. Now it has become official, so once some dollars are added into the USD reserve, they can't be used to buy BTC anymore.

> compute gets allocated to 3 places: training, serving customers and the research team.

so damn cool.

tweets.text:
The reason that Robinhood Chain is so exciting is _not just because it is an Arbitrum rollup_ although that _is very cool_.  

It is cool because of distribution. Over 27 million registered Robinhood users will get access to the chain.  

Every new Fintech company that adopts crypto brings its entire userbase with it.  

This is why its SO important to have Fintech companies adopt Ethereum/Rollups/Real Crypto. Not an intranet-like blockchain.   

The Institutional Era is not just about people 'buying crypto' but their ability to distribute millions of users into DeFi. 

We have spent 10+ years getting ready for this moment. 

Slowly, then suddenly.
